sections.heading: World
bullets: Lebanon has begun peace talks with Israel, though the Lebanese government holds limited influence over the involved groups.
Pope Leo XIV has commenced an 11-day tour of Africa, starting with a papal visit to Algeria, signaling the continent's growing significance to the Catholic Church.
Spain's Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez's wife, Begoña Gómez, has been charged with corruption following a two-year investigation into her use of her relationship to advance her career.

sections.heading: Australia
bullets: Colombian officials have approved a plan to cull up to 80 hippos, descendants of those brought to the country by drug lord Pablo Escobar.
A man has been charged with the murder of his pregnant ex-partner, Hayley McClenahan-Ernst, nine years after her suspicious death in Sydney's west.
The WA government is considering converting the unused Bullsbrook COVID quarantine facility into a prison due to an 'unprecedented' rise in inmate numbers.

sections.heading: Economics
bullets: OpenAI's investors are questioning its $852 billion valuation as CEO Sam Altman shifts the company's strategy amid competition.
China's economy is undergoing a significant transformation, with a surge in high-tech goods driven by intense competition, subsidies, and scale, impacting global advanced industries.
Oil prices have retreated following reports of renewed talks between Iran and the US, though Iran states it will only accept discussions within international law.
